title: Clidemia eggersii
taxonomy: Clidemia eggersii is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to Ecuador. Within the family Melastomataceae, Clidemia eggersii is classified as a member of the tribe Miconieae. The exact classification of Clidemia eggersii within the family Melastomataceae is further divided into genera and species, but it is known to be closely related to other species within the Melastomataceae family. The classification of Clidemia eggersii can be found in the International Plant Names Index or other botanical databases.

intro: Clidemia eggersii, also known as the Puerto Rican clidemia, is a plant species native to the island of Puerto Rico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, infections, and various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Clidemia eggersii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
